spoilers.title: ⚠️ #ONEPIECE1188 DETAILED SPOILERS⚠️ Luffy calls Loki his "buddy"! ------------------------------------ Imu and Luffy continues to fight Imu does the weird hand gestures again and fires a barrage of O
spoilers.text: ⚠️ #ONEPIECE1188 DETAILED SPOILERS⚠️ Luffy calls Loki his "buddy"! ------------------------------------ Imu and Luffy continues to fight Imu does the weird hand gestures again and fires a barrage of Omen flames at Luffy. Luffy runs at full speed in classic Gear 5 style, LOL. The Omen flames home in on Luffy, and his eyes bulge out in surprise, Gear 5 style. Luffy: Woah! It's chasing me! Those things are bombs, aren't they?! GOMU GOMU NO... JET GATLING!!! 👊💢 Luffy punches the Omen flames, and they explode. Luffy: GOMU GOMU NO... MAJIN!!! (魔神. Genie)* Just a point to note: while Majin (魔神) is commonly translated as "genie," its Kanjis literally mean "Demon God" or "Evil God" Luffy transforms into a giant genie. Luffy: HOHOHOHOHO!!! It's not like I wasn't watching Loki's fight, you know! I won't let his "death" be in vain! Loki: I'm still alive, you fool!!! *Cough! Wheeze!* (😂😂😂) Luffy: Oh, so you were alive, Loki?! Imu fires Omen bombs at Loki. Loki: Crap! Dammit! Ragnir gets in front of Loki and tries to shield him. Ragnir: Kuriririri!!! Loki: Get out of the way, Ragnir! Jinbe appears and repels the Omen attack. Jinbe: Arabesque Brick Fist!!! Luffy, if you're gonna ask me to protect this guy, then I'll do it... Luffy: Thank goodness!! I'm counting on you, Jinbe!! He's my buddy*!! Luffy calls Loki "ダチ" (dachi), a very casual slang term for "friend," similar to saying "buddy" or "pal" in English. Loki: Who are you calling your buddy, you little runt?! Loki's speaking in a low voice, because he's heavily injured. Jinbe: Alright, leave it to me! Focus on your fight! Ragnir is looking with awe at Jinbei, obviously grateful to him for protecting Loki.

spoilers.title: ⚠️ #ONEPIECE1188 DETAILED SPOILERS ⚠️ At the beginning of the chapter, Imu remembers his first conversation with Joyboy. They were talking over the phone (although no image is shown, it was most likel
spoilers.text: ⚠️ #ONEPIECE1188 DETAILED SPOILERS ⚠️ At the beginning of the chapter, Imu remembers his first conversation with Joyboy. They were talking over the phone (although no image is shown, it was most likely through a Den Den Mushi—though who knows what their mode of communication was back in the Void Century? LOL) because they were saying "Moshi, moshi?" which is the Japanese equivalent of answering the phone with "Hello?" Beginning Note: "Reminiscing about that day..." Joyboy: Hello? Hello! Come in! Imu: *Zzzzt...* Hello? Joyboy: Hm? Huh? What? Who are you? Imu: I am Imu!! Who art thou?! (It's funnier in Japanese because Imu said "Mu wa Imu!" - "I am Imu!" LOL) Joyboy: Oh, me? I'm... Joyboy!!! (We can only see the lower part of Joyboy's face in Imu's reminiscence—his nose, mouth, and chin.) Back to the present: Imu (to Luffy): Indeed... Joyboy is already dead... and yet... why are you making me remember that?!

misc.title: #ONEPIECE1188 Dude just arrived and kicked already the ass of the strongest characters on that island😭😭 Worst part I legit think Imu still is not much serious, just his basic showcase of powers like
misc.text: #ONEPIECE1188 Dude just arrived and kicked already the ass of the strongest characters on that island😭😭 Worst part I legit think Imu still is not much serious, just his basic showcase of powers like how Luffy would fight just in Base form or using G2...

misc.title: #ONEPIECE1188 Imu took things very personally and was furious with Luffy, likely because, in his eyes, Luffy is weak and is dragging Joyboy’s name and honor through the mud. It makes you wonder just h
misc.text: #ONEPIECE1188 Imu took things very personally and was furious with Luffy, likely because, in his eyes, Luffy is weak and is dragging Joyboy’s name and honor through the mud. It makes you wonder just how insanely powerful Imu and Joyboy actually are... damn.

misc.title: Aight! Lets talk about it The premiere genuinely felt like watching an episode that came out just a week after #170. Recaps were tastefully placed in the middle of dialogues and not used for padding.
